summary: LinkedIn Posts API commentary is little-text format: raw # is a reserved character, so plain #hashtags silently render as non-hashtags. Hashtags must be sent as {hashtag|\#|Tag} templates and other reserved chars backslash-escaped. Also: --replace-existing deletes the old post and its engagement; prefer LinkedIn UI edit for visible-text fixes on engaged posts. publish-article skill script patched 2026-07-10 with the conversion plus a dry-run safety assert.

lesson: Published IR baseline numbers are a cheap harness-correctness oracle: reproducing BEIR BM25 references within a few percent (english analyzer, best_fields multi_match over title/text with tie_breaker 0.5, linear-gain nDCG) validated an entire new evaluation harness in one run, and the mission text pre-declaring 'far-off numbers mean harness bug, not discovery' prevented false-positive celebration.
why: New evaluation harnesses fail silently; external reference numbers turn silent failure into a visible diff.
how_to_apply: When building any evaluator for a public benchmark, embed the published reference number in the output artifact and report the delta automatically.

lesson: Check dataset archive sizes from external sources (an HTTP HEAD request gives content-length) before downloading; intuition about sizes is unreliable - the CQADupStack archive is 4.98 GB, five times MS MARCO's, while the multi-million-document Tier 3 corpora all download at around one gigabyte or less.
why: A blind download hung twice and wasted an hour; one HEAD request per dataset would have shaped the whole execution plan up front.
how_to_apply: Record download sizes in the dataset registry before fetching; use curl-to-disk with retries plus streaming extraction for anything near in-memory buffer limits.

lesson: Never edit a script while a detached background job may still exec it: a streaming refactor of the OpenSearch loader landed mid-run and one CQADupStack forum loaded a hybrid half-edited script, producing a silently corrupted baseline (0 counted docs, nDCG@10 0.0567 vs the expected ~0.27).
why: Detached jobs re-read script files at each node invocation, so an edit mid-loop splits the run across two script versions.
how_to_apply: Freeze scripts used by running detached jobs; stage refactors in a copy, or wait for the job to finish. Always sanity-check per-item load counts (0 counted docs is a red flag even when the evaluation returns nonzero scores).

lesson: Root-cause correction to LES-013: the corrupted forum baseline came from TWO concurrent copies of the same load-evaluate-drop loop racing on shared index names - a replacement loop was started after a liveness check that sampled between child processes and wrongly concluded the original had died. Verify detached-job liveness by the parent shell PID (or a pidfile), never by grepping for transient child process names, and make replacement runs idempotent or lock-guarded.
why: Load-evaluate-drop pipelines mutate shared remote state; a duplicate run silently drops the index the other copy is evaluating, producing plausible-looking but corrupted numbers.
how_to_apply: Record the parent PID when detaching; before restarting, kill -0 that PID and check its process group. Prefer one orchestrator that resumes from written artifacts over launching parallel replacements.

lesson: Harden every remote-call path symmetrically, not just the obvious one. The bulk-load path got retry/backoff after NQ died mid-index, but the evaluation path did not - so a single transient fetch failure during FEVER's 6,666-query evaluation aborted the whole run with no artifact, after the 5.42M-doc index had already been built and was then dropped, forcing a full re-index. A retry wrapper on the shared search helper (offline-eval only, never the runtime Worker path) fixes it.
why: Long offline jobs against a stressed free-tier instance will hit transient errors on ANY call; an unhardened path is a single point of failure that wastes the most expensive work (the completed index).
how_to_apply: When adding retry logic for one remote operation, audit sibling operations in the same job for the same failure mode. Keep retry wrappers in the script/lib layer, out of the runtime serving path.
